pattern: A bowl is bounded by two peaks that both tower over the valley between them, so a decreasing monotonic stack reveals every qualifying end pair in one pass.
hints: A bowl needs both endpoints strictly greater than every element strictly inside it.
Maintain a decreasing stack while scanning; a smaller element trapped between two taller ones is the floor of a bowl.

Sweep left to right with a stack that keeps elements in decreasing order; whenever the incoming element is taller you pop the shorter top, and if a taller element still remains beneath it that popped element was the valley of exactly one bowl bounded on the left by the new stack top and on the right by the incoming element.

edge_inputs.description: descending then a tail forms nested bowls

stress.description: large permutation of distinct values to stress the stack
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    arr = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(arr)
    return [arr]
